title: dispatch_peer_deliver calls get_chats then insert_message_ext — two round trips to store per incoming message
description: daemon/src/relay.rs dispatch_peer_deliver: first calls store.get_chats(Some(&[chat_id.as_str()])) to verify the chat exists, then calls store.insert_message_ext(...). This is two DB round trips per peer-delivered message. insert_message_ext with a FK constraint on chat_id would fail with a FK violation if the chat does not exist — the caller could simply attempt the insert and handle the FK error. The pre-check adds latency on the hot path (every inbound peer message) and the SQLite FK enforcement already enforces the invariant. For high-volume messaging this doubles DB I/O per message.
